tokens: I'm at the foot of the ladder.

transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: I'm going to step off the LM now.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: That's one small step for (a) man; one giant leap for mankind.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: Yes, the surface is fine and powdery.
I can kick it up loosely with my toe.
It does adhere in fine layers, like powdered charcoal, to the sole and sides of my boots.
I only go in a small fraction of an inch, maybe an eighth of an inch, but I can see the footprints of my boots and the treads in the fine, sandy particles.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: There seems to be no difficulty in moving around - as we suspected.
It's even perhaps easier than the simulations of one-sixth g that we performed in the various simulations on the ground.
It's absolutely no trouble to walk around.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: The descent engine did not leave a crater of any size.
It has about one foot clearance on the ground.
We're essentially on a very level place here.
I can see some evidence of rays emanating from the descent engine, but a very insignificant amount.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: Okay, Buzz, we ready to bring down the (
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: I think it's been all squared away and in good shape.

tokens: You'll have to pay out all the LEC.
It looks like it's coming out nice and evenly.

tokens: It's quite dark here in the shadow and a little hard for me to see that I have good footing.

tokens: I think you're pulling the wrong one.

tokens: I'm ready to pull it down now.
There was still a little bit left in the (LEC bag)...

tokens: Don't hold it quite so tight.

tokens: Looking up at the LM...I'm standing directly in the shadow now, looking up at Buzz in the windows.

tokens: It's not all the way in.

tokens: Back in (garbled) (Pause) Now a little of foot movement (garbled) porch.

tokens: That's our home for the next couple of hours and we want to take good care of it.

tokens: I found I could be very comfortable, and walking is also very comfortable.

tokens: For those who haven't read the plaque, we'll read the plaque that's on the front landing gear of this LM.
First there's two hemispheres, one showing each of the two hemispheres of the Earth.
Underneath it says "Here Men from the planet Earth first set foot upon the Moon, July 1969 A.D.
We came in peace for all mankind." It has the crew members' signatures and the signature of the President of the United States.

tokens: No, you take this TV on out.

Now you're going too fast on the panorama sweep.
You're going to have to stop for...

tokens: I haven't stopped...I haven't set it down yet.

tokens: Tell me if you've got a picture, Houston.

tokens: We've got a beautiful picture, Neil.

tokens: Now, this one is right down-Sun, straight west.

transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: ...large angular rock in the foreground, and it looks like a much smaller rock a couple of inches to the left of it.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And then on beyond it about 10 feet is an even larger rock that's very rounded.
(Pause) That rock is about...The closest one to you is sticking out of the sand about one foot.
And it's about a foot and one-half long, and it's about six inches thick, but it's standing on edge.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: I've got the table out (and) got a bag deployed.

tokens: And we see the shadow of the LM.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The little hill just beyond the shadow of the LM is a pair of elongate craters about...
Probably the pair together is about 40 feet long (east-west) and 20 feet across (north-south), and they're probably 6 feet deep.
We'll probably get some more work in there later.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: We see Buzz going about his work (with the SWC).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: For a final orientation, we'd like it to come left about 5 degrees.
(Pause) Now back to the right about half as much.

tokens: You can make a mark, Houston.

tokens: And, incidentally, you can use the shadow that the staff makes to assist you getting it perpendicular (to the Sun line) (garbled)

tokens: Some of these small depressions (garbled) soft and you tend to sink, oh, maybe 2 or 3 inches.
(Garbled) suggest exactly what the Surveyor pictures showed when they pushed away a little bit.

tokens: (Aproaching the MESA) I noticed in the soft spots where we have footprints nearly an inch deep that the soil is very cohesive; and it will retain a slope of probably 70 degrees along the side of the footprints.
